accepted.reason: Golomb bitstream encode/decode logic is a compact entropy-coding kernel with bit-level operations.
Projected-gradient coordinate updates for constrained NMF are strong matrix-optimization primitives.
Buffered read loops with completion flags represent practical concurrent I/O workflow patterns.
Flat ragged-array storage with start/end indexing is a useful data-layout kernel.
Policy-tree value aggregation over transition and observation probabilities is a strong planning kernel.
Typed integer pack/unpack routines are practical low-level serialization kernels.
Dijkstra shortest-path over adjacency matrix is a canonical graph-algorithm optimization workload.
Trilinear interpolation over 4D arrays provides high-signal numeric indexing and weighting logic.

skipped.reason: OpenGL/SDL texture-loading binding code is integration-heavy and not a standalone conversion kernel.
Cython cfunc conversion behavior test rather than domain algorithmic kernel code.
